reasoning: simpleGet is the default export of index.js (module.exports = simpleGet) and is fully public. The redirect-following behavior is enabled by default (maxRedirects: 10 is set as a default merge, not an opt-in). No non-default option is required to reach the vulnerable path: any 3xx response from any server causes res.headers.location to be assigned to opts.url and passed recursively into simpleGet() without any validation of the new URL against private IP ranges, allowlists, or schema checks. The only mitigations present are (1) maxRedirects counting (prevents infinite loops, not SSRF) and (2) dropping cookie/authorization headers on host change (prevents credential leakage to the internal target but does not block the request itself). No input validation (joi, zod, ajv, isPrivateIP, allowedRedirect) is present anywhere in the redirect handler. The vulnerable parameter (res.headers.location) is entirely attacker-controlled when the attacker can influence the HTTP server that the caller contacts. This is default-reachable with zero non-default options required.
